FT_IC1.evidence: U.S. ABCD Study, N=1,415 youth, ages ~14, cross-sectional passive sensing via EARS application

FT_IC5.evidence: Original empirical analysis of ABCD passive sensing data with validation of smartphone use measures

FT_IC6.evidence: Published peer-reviewed paper in Scientific Reports 2024

FT_IC2.evidence: Pure psychometric/validation paper (§2.4.6 binding clarification). Describes and validates EARS keyboard and app use measures; reports correlations between passive and self-report measures (r=0.21-0.35); no analysis pairs smartphone use (IV or DV) with individual-trait outcome. Measurement-focused rather than outcome-focused.

notes: Paper is a validation study of passive sensing methods applied to ABCD data, not an association study.

FT_IC1.evidence: Describes U.S. ABCD Study infrastructure and community support; U.S.-based, 11,000+ youth

FT_IC5.evidence: Resource/infrastructure paper. Describes NowIKnowMyABCD platform, GitHub discussion board, and website for ABCD community support. Does not report original empirical analysis of ABCD data. Non-empirical per §2.2 (resource/data-availability description).

notes: Excluded under FT-EC3: Non-empirical paper (resource platform description).

FT_IC1.evidence: U.S. ABCD Study, N=11,868 participants, ages ~9/10 to ~13/14, waves 0-4 longitudinal

FT_IC5.evidence: Original empirical analysis: longitudinal and cross-sectional patterns of technology use; multilevel logistic regression; descriptive statistics and covariate associations

FT_IC6.evidence: Published peer-reviewed original research, Journal of Adolescent Health 2024

FT_IC2.evidence: Step 1: No phone/SM-specific estimate paired with individual-trait outcome. Paper measures hours/day of TV, social media, texting, video chat, video games, YouTube as technology use variables. Step 2 not applicable (no phone/SM-specific estimate in Step 1). Other-side of all analyses is demographic-only: biological sex, parent education, parent income, parent marital status. Per binding rule §2.4: "Demographic-only (age, sex, race/ethnicity, household income, parental education, household composition, marital status) on the other side → NOT_MET." No individual-trait outcome paired with technology variables.

notes: Excluded under FT-EC2: Technology use patterns analyzed only in association with sociodemographic variables; no individual-trait-level outcome (neural, behavioral, health, cognitive, well-being, personality, substance use, sleep, academic, psychiatric, etc.).
